verbatim_text: For the first part about parametrization, we said LLMs can represent algorithms and they can perform test time adaptation by sampling more tokens. For the second part, we equated the notion of learning algorithms with solving a meta RL problem. And what that meant is that it gave us this objective, which has these two parts, sampling a trace and rewarding a trace. And now we need to go and figure out these two parts in the next part of the talk. But before that, let me actually pause here for questions, if there are any. Let me continue that. So in the next part, we'll look at some practical design choices for these two. But let's say I give you some Oracle options for the sample and reward. These are really good values. How do we learn an algorithm? That's fairly simple. Now you have these two quantities nailed down. All we need to do is maximize this objective. And for that, we typically use some iterative optimization procedures in reinforcement learning.
